message:
test(ft-h8v8v): libfuzzer harness for WireEnvelope distributed-mode wire path

Adds fuzz/fuzz_targets/wire_envelope.rs targeting WireEnvelope::from_json
+ Aggregator::ingest, the entry points where untrusted network bytes
flow into frankenterm-core when distributed mode is enabled. A panic,
OOM, stack overflow, or deserialization confusion on this boundary is
a remote-DoS surface — pre-fix, the only test coverage was
proptest_wire_protocol.rs which round-trips well-formed structs, not
adversarial bytes.

## Contracts pinned by the harness

1. **No panic on any byte sequence.** WireEnvelope::from_json AND
   Aggregator::ingest must return Ok or Err for every input — never
   trip a debug-assert, integer overflow panic, or stack overflow on
   deeply-nested JSON.
2. **Saturating-counter invariant.** The Aggregator's saturating_add
   semantics on total_accepted / total_rejected: the sequence-mode
   path drives N consecutive ingests against one aggregator and
   asserts agent_count() <= max_agents and that the counter reads
   are panic-free.

## Modes (Arbitrary-driven)

- Single: feeds one byte slice through both from_json (parser-only)
  AND Aggregator::ingest (parser + state). Hits malformed UTF-8,
  deeply-nested arrays, integer overflow attempts on version/seq,
  adversarial payload variants.
- Sequence: drives one Aggregator through up to 32 raw-bytes ingests,
  letting libFuzzer minimize toward inputs that hit the eviction +
  duplicate-seq + future-clock branches. Bounded to 16 max_agents
  and 1 MiB per input to keep the campaign tight.

## Verified
- cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin wire_envelope
  → exit 0, no warnings on the harness file. (Full cargo build of
  the fuzz crate fails only in transitive openssl-sys C build,
  unrelated to this harness.)

Future work / not in this commit:
- Long-running campaign — `cargo +nightly fuzz run wire_envelope --
  -runs=1000` or `-max_total_time=600` for the actual hunt.
- Corpus seeding from existing valid envelopes captured via the
  to_json round-trip in proptest_wire_protocol.rs.

Mirrors the harness shape used by ipc_auth_envelope.rs (Archetype 5
structure-aware via Arbitrary + Archetype 1 crash detector).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -54,6 +54,17 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/pattern_pack_toml.rs"
 doc = false
 required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
 
+# ft-8hbq8: YAML+JSON sibling of pattern_pack_toml. Targets the
+# attacker-reachable `.ft/patterns/*.{yaml,json}` deserialize path
+# that the loader at patterns.rs:1203 dispatches to via the file
+# extension. Drives the same PatternPack deserializer + validation
+# gate as the production loader, minus the filesystem read.
+[[bin]]
+name = "pattern_pack_loader"
+path = "fuzz_targets/pattern_pack_loader.rs"
+doc = false
+required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "pattern_detect_with_context_streaming"
 path = "fuzz_targets/pattern_detect_with_context_streaming.rs"
@@ -114,6 +125,12 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/scan_pipeline_chunked.rs"
 doc = false
 required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "simd_scan"
+path = "fuzz_targets/simd_scan.rs"
+doc = false
+required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "tuning_config_toml"
 path = "fuzz_targets/tuning_config_toml.rs"
@@ -210,6 +227,12 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/ipc_auth_envelope.rs"
 doc = false
 required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "wire_envelope"
+path = "fuzz_targets/wire_envelope.rs"
+doc = false
+required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "escape_parser_raw"
 path = "fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs"
